posture: Quiet Wednesday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the §1a-§1d free sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Several recurring or previously-considered items were again reviewed and excluded as out-of-window, out-of-scope or duplicative: Townsville City Council's AI pedestrian-detection waste-truck safety system (an 'Australian first', but dated June 2026) is prior-period background rather than a fresh 24-hour signal; recycled-content road material surfaced only via old or undated coverage (a Sutherland Shire foamed-asphalt 'recycled roads' trial resurfacing in search results is a 2020 article; City of Parramatta's Reconophalt resurfacing carries no fresh dated development); the NSW AI-in-planning activity (multi-council Early Adopter trial, DAISY development-application assistant) and the NSW statewide FOGO mandate (large-business phase from 1 July) produced no new dated development today and are rollout dates rather than fresh innovation. Overseas material (Bloomberg CityLab / Mayors Challenge 2025-26 winners, UK Simpler Recycling and Local Government Reorganisation waste coverage via LGIU, LGA and MRW, smart-waste vendor blogs) was either out-of-window, out-of-scope, or general policy/network content rather than a fresh, council-transferable signal. Today is a Wednesday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
